expression: I wanted to give you a heads up
naturalMeaning: 提前告知对方某事，让对方有心理准备。美国人在工作交流中经常用这个引出即将发生的事情或潜在问题。
example: I wanted to give you a heads up — the client is going to ask about the timeline tomorrow.
exampleTranslation: 我想提前告诉你一声，客户明天会问时间表的事。

expression: Here's where we're at
naturalMeaning: 向对方汇报当前进展状态。非常口语化的更新开场，比'Here is the current status'自然得多。
example: Here's where we're at — we finished the backend work and we're starting the UI layer this week.
exampleTranslation: 这是目前的进展：后端做完了，这周开始搞UI层。

expression: We're on track to
naturalMeaning: 表示项目按计划推进，有望按时完成某个目标。美国人常用这个来表达进度正常。
example: We're on track to ship the beta by end of month.
exampleTranslation: 我们按计划可以在月底前发布beta版本。

expression: Walk me through where things stand
literalTranslation: 给我过一遍目前的状况
naturalMeaning: 请对方详细说明当前进展。'walk me through'是非常自然的美国口语，比'explain to me'更地道。
example: Can you walk me through where things stand on the migration project?
exampleTranslation: 你能跟我过一遍迁移项目的当前状况吗？

expression: Just an FYI
naturalMeaning: FYI是'For Your Information'的缩写，表示只是通知你一下，不需要回复或行动。日常工作中每天都在用。
example: Just an FYI — the deploy window got pushed to Thursday.
exampleTranslation: 只是跟你说一下，部署窗口推到周四了。

expression: We're running into some headwinds
naturalMeaning: 用'headwinds'(逆风)比喻遇到了阻力或困难，比直接说'problems'更委婉专业。美国人特别喜欢用这个商业隐喻。
example: We're running into some headwinds with the API integration — the vendor changed their spec on us.
exampleTranslation: 我们在API集成上遇到了一些阻力，供应商单方面改了他们的接口规范。

expression: Things are moving along
naturalMeaning: 表达进展顺利但并不急于结束的状态，非常自然的日常口语说法。
example: Things are moving along — we're a little behind on testing but nothing we can't make up.
exampleTranslation: 事情在推进中，测试稍微落后了一点，但能赶上来。

expression: Give me a progress bar
naturalMeaning: 形象地让对方用百分比或其他可量化的方式说明进度。科技行业特别常用这种比喻。
example: Give me a progress bar on where you are with the Q3 planning.
exampleTranslation: 你Q3规划做到哪了，给我个进度百分比。

expression: Let me set expectations upfront
literalTranslation: 我先提前把预期说清楚
naturalMeaning: 在开始之前坦诚地告诉对方可以合理期待什么。这是经理和项目负责人常用的开场。
example: Let me set expectations upfront — we're not going to have a full prototype until next sprint.
exampleTranslation: 我先提前说清楚，到下个sprint之前我们不会有完整原型。

expression: I want to be transparent with you
naturalMeaning: 在说实话或传达坏消息之前用的铺垫，表示接下来要说真话而不是藏着掖着。美国人高度重视'transparency'。
example: I want to be transparent with you — we underestimated the scope and we're going to need two more weeks.
exampleTranslation: 我想跟你坦诚说，我们低估了工作量，还需要两周时间。

expression: Under-promise and over-deliver
naturalMeaning: 一种管理期望的策略：故意把预期设低，然后交付超出预期的结果。这是美国职场的一句经典格言。
example: I try to under-promise and over-deliver with stakeholders instead of over-committing.
exampleTranslation: 我尽量对干系人少承诺多交付，而不是过度承诺。

expression: Let me level with you
naturalMeaning: 坦诚相待，放下外交辞令。'level with someone'意思是跟对方开诚布公，不说客套话。
example: Let me level with you — if we don't get this funding round, we're going to have to make cuts.
exampleTranslation: 我跟你说实话，如果这轮融资拿不到，我们就得裁员了。

expression: Here's what you can realistically expect
literalTranslation: 这是你可以实际期待的
naturalMeaning: 在对方期待过高时温和地拉回现实，给出切实可行的预期。
example: Here's what you can realistically expect — the first version will have the core features but not the bells and whistles.
exampleTranslation: 这是你实际可以期待的：第一版会有核心功能，但花里胡哨的东西不会有。

expression: Don't get your hopes up
naturalMeaning: 提醒对方不要期望过高以免失望。语气比较直接，通常在熟人之间使用。
example: Don't get your hopes up about the launch date — we've missed our last three deadlines.
exampleTranslation: 别对上线日期抱太大希望，我们已经连续错过三个截止日期了。

expression: I just want to level-set here
literalTranslation: 我只是想把这些都摆平
naturalMeaning: 让所有人对当前状况有一致的认知和理解。'level-set'很常见，尤其在高管会议中。
example: I just want to level-set here — everyone agreed to the Q3 timeline in April, so that's what we're working with.
exampleTranslation: 我想让大家对齐一下认知：四月份所有人都同意了Q3的时间表，所以我们现在按这个在执行。

expression: That's not going to work for us
naturalMeaning: 礼貌但坚定地拒绝一个提议。不会说'No'这么直接，但意思很清楚。标准的职场拒绝方式。
example: I appreciate you suggesting that timeline, but that's not going to work for us given our current capacity.
exampleTranslation: 感谢你提出这个时间表，但以我们目前的容量来说行不通。

expression: We just don't have the bandwidth
literalTranslation: 我们就是没有这个带宽
naturalMeaning: 用'bandwidth'(带宽)比喻团队的时间和精力容量不足。这是科技行业最经典的拒绝理由之一。
example: We'd love to help with that initiative, but we just don't have the bandwidth right now.
exampleTranslation: 我们很愿意支持那个项目，但现在真没有这个余力。

expression: I'm going to have to push back on that
literalTranslation: 我不得不在那个事情上反对一下
naturalMeaning: 礼貌地表示反对或拒绝。'push back'比'say no'更柔和，是职场中很常见的表达异议方式。
example: I'm going to have to push back on that deadline — there's no way we can finish QA in one week.
exampleTranslation: 我不得不在这个截止日期上表示异议，我们不可能一周内完成QA。

expression: That's off the table
literalTranslation: 那件事不在讨论范围内了
naturalMeaning: 明确表示某个选项不再可行或不再考虑。非常自然的商业表达。
example: A full rewrite is off the table — we don't have the time or budget for that.
exampleTranslation: 完全重写不可能了，我们没有那个时间和预算。

expression: I'd love to help, but I've got my hands full
literalTranslation: 我很愿意帮忙，但我手头已经满了
naturalMeaning: 用友好的语气拒绝帮忙请求。'have my hands full'是非常生动的口语表达，说自己忙得手都占满了。
example: I'd love to help with that proposal, but I've got my hands full with the audit right now.
exampleTranslation: 我很愿意帮你搞那个方案，但我现在正忙着审计呢。

expression: That's not really in scope
naturalMeaning: 用项目范围来委婉拒绝新增需求。'in scope/out of scope'是项目管理的基本术语。
example: Redesigning the login flow isn't really in scope for this sprint — let's add it to the backlog.
exampleTranslation: 重新设计登录流程不在这个sprint范围内，我们先放到待办列表里。

expression: That's a non-starter
naturalMeaning: 表示某个提议一开始就不可能实现，完全没有讨论的必要。语气比较强，但很地道。
example: Shipping to customers without any testing is a non-starter for me.
exampleTranslation: 不经任何测试就发给客户，这在我这里根本不可能。

expression: I can't commit to that right now
naturalMeaning: 在不想说死又不能直接拒绝时使用。'commit to'在美国职场中非常常用，表示正式承诺。
example: I can't commit to that right now — let me check with the team and get back to you.
exampleTranslation: 我现在不能承诺这个，让我跟团队确认后再回复你。

expression: I need to get your buy-in on this
literalTranslation: 我需要你在这个事情上买进
naturalMeaning: 请求对方支持或认可一个方案。'buy-in'是美国职场核心词汇，指利益相关者的认可和支持。
example: Before we move forward, I need to get your buy-in on the budget allocation.
exampleTranslation: 在推进之前，我需要你同意这个预算分配方案。

expression: Can I get you on board?
naturalMeaning: 询问对方是否愿意支持某个计划。'get on board'比喻上同一艘船朝着共同目标前进。
example: I'm proposing we restructure the team. Can I get you on board with this?
exampleTranslation: 我提议重组团队，你能支持这个方案吗？

expression: Hear me out
naturalMeaning: 在对方可能不认同或准备打断时请求对方先听完你的整个想法。非常自然的日常表达。
example: I know this sounds crazy, but hear me out — what if we launch without the analytics dashboard?
exampleTranslation: 我知道这听起来很疯狂，但听我说完——如果我们不上分析仪表板就发布呢？

expression: I want to loop you in early
naturalMeaning: 在早期阶段就邀请对方参与讨论，让对方有参与感和主人翁意识。'loop someone in'意思是把某人加入沟通圈。
example: I want to loop you in early on this initiative so you have a chance to shape it.
exampleTranslation: 我想早点把你拉进来，让你有机会参与这个项目的方向。

expression: I need an ally on this one
literalTranslation: 我在这件事上需要一个盟友
naturalMeaning: 在需要寻求他人支持时坦诚表达自己的需求。'ally'比'supporter'更有深度，暗示需要一起努力。
example: I need an ally on this one — I'm going to need someone to back me up in the exec meeting.
exampleTranslation: 我在这件事上需要一个盟友，在高管会上需要有人支持我。

expression: I think you'll like where I'm going with this
literalTranslation: 我觉得你会喜欢我要说的方向
naturalMeaning: 在推销想法之前先用正面暗示勾起对方的兴趣。很自然的说服技巧。
example: I think you'll like where I'm going with this — instead of hiring more engineers, we automate the whole pipeline.
exampleTranslation: 我觉得你会喜欢我的思路——我们不招更多工程师，而是把整个流程自动化。

expression: I need to win hearts and minds
naturalMeaning: 表示不仅需要对方的形式同意，更需要情感上的认同和支持。常用于组织变革或文化转型。
example: This reorg isn't going to work unless I win hearts and minds across the team.
exampleTranslation: 这个重组只有赢得整个团队的真心认同才能成功。

expression: Let me walk you through the rationale
literalTranslation: 让我带你过一遍背后的逻辑
naturalMeaning: 详细解释做出某个决定的理由和逻辑。'rationale'比'reason'更正式专业。
example: Let me walk you through the rationale behind choosing AWS over GCP for this.
exampleTranslation: 让我解释一下选择AWS而不是GCP背后的考虑。

expression: There's no easy way to say this
literalTranslation: 没有轻松的说法能说这个
naturalMeaning: 在传达坏消息之前的惯用开场白，让对方做好心理准备。这是美剧中也很常见的表达。
example: There's no easy way to say this — we lost the account.
exampleTranslation: 这很难开口，我们丢了这个客户。

expression: I'm going to level with you
naturalMeaning: 在坦诚对话前表明态度，意味着接下来不讲客套话。比'I'm going to be honest'更有冲击力和诚意。
example: I'm going to level with you — I don't think this product is going to meet its revenue targets.
exampleTranslation: 我跟你说实话，我觉得这个产品达不到收入目标。

expression: Let's not sugarcoat this
literalTranslation: 我们别给这个裹糖衣了
naturalMeaning: 不要粉饰太平，不要美化事实。'sugarcoat'是比喻性动词，非常生动形象。
example: Let's not sugarcoat this — we missed the deadline because of poor planning, not because of external factors.
exampleTranslation: 我们别粉饰了，这次错过截止日期是因为计划不周，不是外部原因。

expression: I need to address the elephant in the room
literalTranslation: 我需要指出房间里的大象
naturalMeaning: 指出大家都心知肚明但没人愿意谈的问题。这是美国职场非常著名的习语。
example: I think we need to address the elephant in the room — our product has serious retention issues.
exampleTranslation: 我觉得我们需要面对那个大家都回避的问题：我们的产品存在严重的留存问题。

expression: That's not what I'm hearing
naturalMeaning: 礼貌地表达异议，表示自己了解的是不同的情况。比直接说'that's wrong'温和得多。
example: You're saying the project is on schedule, but that's not what I'm hearing from the engineering team.
exampleTranslation: 你说项目按计划进行，但我从工程团队那里听到的不是这样。

expression: I know this isn't what you wanted to hear
literalTranslation: 我知道这不是你想听的
naturalMeaning: 在传达坏消息时表达同理心，承认对方的失望。非常有情商的说法。
example: I know this isn't what you wanted to hear, but the launch is getting pushed to next quarter.
exampleTranslation: 我知道这不是你想听的，但上线要推迟到下个季度了。

expression: Let's not dance around this
literalTranslation: 我们别在这个事情上绕圈子了
naturalMeaning: 鼓励直接面对问题，不要绕弯子。'dance around'形象地表示像跳舞一样闪躲回避。
example: Let's not dance around this — we all know the feature isn't going to be ready on time.
exampleTranslation: 我们别绕圈子了，大家都知道这个功能没法按时完成。

expression: I'm not trying to point fingers
naturalMeaning: 在讨论问题之前先声明不是为了甩锅追责，而是为了解决问题。非常实用的职场表达。
example: I'm not trying to point fingers here — I just want us to figure out how to avoid this next time.
exampleTranslation: 我不是要指责谁，我只是想搞清楚下次怎么避免这个问题。

expression: I'll keep this high-level
literalTranslation: 我会保持在这个高层面
naturalMeaning: 表示只讲概要，不深入细节。汇报给高管时标配用语，因为他们不想听细枝末节。
example: I'll keep this high-level and save the technical details for the follow-up doc.
exampleTranslation: 我讲个大概，技术细节留在后续文档里。

expression: Here's the bottom line
naturalMeaning: 告诉对方最核心的结论或结果。高管们最喜欢听的就是'bottom line'。
example: Here's the bottom line — we need to cut costs by 15% this quarter or we won't hit our margin targets.
exampleTranslation: 核心问题是：本季度必须削减15%的成本，否则达不到利润率目标。

expression: Let me cut to the chase
naturalMeaning: 跳过开场白和客套话，直接讲重点。是美国影视剧和职场中最经典的一种表达。
example: Let me cut to the chase — we need more engineers or we're going to miss every milestone this quarter.
exampleTranslation: 我直说了，我们需要更多工程师，否则本季度每个节点都会延期。

expression: The TL;DR is
naturalMeaning: TL;DR是'Too Long; Didn't Read'的缩写，表示先给一个简短总结。硅谷和科技公司中极其流行。
example: The TL;DR is we're profitable, but growth is slowing down faster than we projected.
exampleTranslation: 总结：我们盈利了，但增长放缓的速度比预期的快。

expression: Here's the one metric you should care about
literalTranslation: 这是你唯一需要关心的指标
naturalMeaning: 在众多数据中提炼出最核心的一个指标给高管看，帮助他们聚焦决策。
example: Here's the one metric you should care about — month-over-month active users is trending down for the third straight month.
exampleTranslation: 这是你唯一需要关心的指标：月活用户已经连续三个月下滑了。

expression: I'll save the details for the appendix
naturalMeaning: 表示不占用会议时间讲细节，但相关资料已准备好可以后续查阅。是给高管做汇报的标准说法。
example: I'll save the technical architecture details for the appendix — for now, just know that the system can handle 10x our current load.
exampleTranslation: 技术架构细节我放到附录里了，现在你只需要知道系统能承受目前10倍的负载。

expression: In a nutshell
naturalMeaning: 用一句话概括复杂的内容，类似于中文的「简而言之」。美国人在总结时非常喜欢用这个比喻。
example: In a nutshell, we're ahead of schedule on engineering but behind on marketing prep.
exampleTranslation: 简单总结：工程进度超前，但市场准备落后了。

expression: Let's zoom out for a second
naturalMeaning: 暂停细节讨论，从更高维度看整体。'zoom out'是数码时代的比喻，非常贴合科技行业的语言习惯。
example: Let's zoom out for a second — are we even building the right thing, or are we just optimizing a feature nobody uses?
exampleTranslation: 我们退一步看大图：我们现在做的东西对吗？还是只是在优化一个没人用的功能？

expression: There's no free lunch
naturalMeaning: 任何选择都有代价。在讨论权衡取舍时经常使用。
example: There's no free lunch here if you want the product to be bug-free we need to push the launch date out.
exampleTranslation: 没有免费的午餐，如果你想要产品没有bug，我们就得推迟上线日期。

expression: It's a trade-off between X and Y
naturalMeaning: 明确列出两个选项之间的利弊。'trade-off'是讨论取舍时的核心词汇。
example: It's a trade-off between speed and quality we can ship next week with fewer features or ship next month with everything.
exampleTranslation: 这需要在速度和质量之间做权衡：下周发布但功能少，或者下个月发布但功能全。

expression: We can't have it all
naturalMeaning: 提醒团队或干系人必须做出取舍，不可能同时满足所有需求。非常口语化的现实提醒。
example: We can't have it all if you want it cheap and fast, it's not going to be good.
exampleTranslation: 我们不能全都要：如果你要便宜又快，那就不会好。

expression: Fast, cheap, or good pick two
naturalMeaning: 经典的三角权衡：速度+质量=贵，质量+便宜=慢，速度+便宜=质量差。在项目管理中无人不知。
example: You want it done by Friday with no bugs and no overtime? Fast, cheap, or good — pick two.
exampleTranslation: 你想周五前完成、没有bug、不用加班？质量、速度、成本，三选二。

expression: Something's gotta give
naturalMeaning: 当资源或时间不够用时，必须放弃某些东西。非常自然的英语习语。
example: With three projects all due next month, something's gotta give.
exampleTranslation: 三个项目都下个月要交付，总得牺牲一个。

expression: We need to pick our battles
naturalMeaning: 不要所有问题都争，要选择值得投入精力的关键事情。非常经典的商业智慧习语。
example: I agree the font choice isn't ideal, but we need to pick our battles — let's focus on the functional issues first.
exampleTranslation: 我同意字体选择不完美，但我们要选好优先级的仗来打，先聚焦功能问题。

expression: The juice isn't worth the squeeze
literalTranslation: 汁水不值得那个挤压的功夫
naturalMeaning: 投入的精力和回报不成正比，不值得做。非常生动形象的美国口语比喻。
example: We could optimize this query to save 50ms, but the juice isn't worth the squeeze honestly.
exampleTranslation: 我们可以优化这个查询节省50毫秒，但说实话不值当的。

expression: Every decision has an opportunity cost
literalTranslation: 每个决定都有机会成本
naturalMeaning: 提醒对方选择了一个方向就意味着放弃了其他可能。是高管们挂在嘴边的说法。
example: If we put all our engineers on the mobile app, the website is going to suffer — every decision has an opportunity cost.
exampleTranslation: 如果所有工程师都去做移动端，网页端就会受影响，每个决定都有机会成本。

expression: How's everything going on your end?
naturalMeaning: 非正式的关心对方近况。美国人之间打招呼用的日常问候，也是建立关系的基础。
example: Hey, we haven't caught up in a while — how's everything going on your end?
exampleTranslation: 嘿，好久没聊了，你那边都还好吗？

expression: I appreciate you looping me in
naturalMeaning: 感谢对方让你参与讨论或把你加入邮件/沟通链。'looping in'在邮件和Slack中非常常用。
example: Thanks, I appreciate you looping me in on this thread — I'll review the proposal by EOD.
exampleTranslation: 谢谢，感谢你把我拉进这个讨论，我今天下班前看完方案。

expression: I value your perspective on this
literalTranslation: 我很看重你在这个事情上的观点
naturalMeaning: 表达对对方意见的尊重，让对方感到被重视。建立人际关系的重要表达。
example: Before I make a final call on the architecture, I want to hear from you because I value your perspective on this.
exampleTranslation: 在我对架构做最终决定之前，我想听听你的意见，因为我很看重你的看法。

expression: Let's grab coffee sometime
literalTranslation: 我们改天一起喝杯咖啡
naturalMeaning: 非正式地约对方聊聊，不一定是真的喝咖啡，更多是一种建立关系的姿态。美国的社交信号。
example: We've been working together for months but barely know each other — let's grab coffee sometime.
exampleTranslation: 我们一起共事好几个月了但互相还不太了解，改天喝杯咖啡聊聊。

expression: I've got your back
naturalMeaning: 表达支持和忠诚，表示会支持对方的决定或在困难时伸出援手。是非常有温度的职场表达。
example: If you want to push back on that requirement in the meeting, go for it — I've got your back.
exampleTranslation: 如果你在会议上想反对那个需求，大胆做，我支持你。

expression: I really appreciate you making the time
literalTranslation: 我真的很感谢你抽出时间
naturalMeaning: 感谢对方在百忙之中抽出时间。尊重别人时间在美国文化中非常重要。
example: I know you have back-to-back meetings today, so I really appreciate you making the time to chat.
exampleTranslation: 我知道你今天会议一个接一个，真的很感谢你抽出时间来聊。

expression: Let me buy you lunch
naturalMeaning: 主动提出请对方吃饭，是建立关系的经典方式。美国人说这个一般是真的会请。
example: You've been a huge help on this project — let me buy you lunch to say thanks.
exampleTranslation: 你在这个项目上帮了大忙，让我请你吃午饭表示感谢。

expression: It's been great working with you on this
naturalMeaning: 项目结束时表达对合作的感谢。真诚、温暖，非常适合作为关系的润滑剂。
example: It's been great working with you on this launch — hope we can team up again soon.
exampleTranslation: 和你一起做这个发布项目很开心，希望很快能再合作。

expression: Here's why this matters to you
literalTranslation: 这是为什么这对你很重要
naturalMeaning: 说服对方的关键招数：把利益和自己的关注点挂钩。'WIIFM (What's In It For Me)'原则的实际应用。
example: I know compliance isn't your favorite topic, but here's why this matters to you — it directly affects how fast we can ship new features.
exampleTranslation: 我知道合规不是你最喜欢的话题，但这对你来说为什么重要呢？它直接影响我们发布新功能的速度。

expression: Let me frame this differently
naturalMeaning: 当对方不同意或不理解时，换一种表述方式来重新说明。非常高级的沟通技巧。
example: You say it's too expensive. Let me frame this differently — what's the cost of not doing it?
exampleTranslation: 你说太贵了，那我换个角度说：不做的成本是什么？

expression: Let me play devil's advocate here
literalTranslation: 让我来扮演唱反调的角色
naturalMeaning: 假装站在反对面的角度来测试一个想法是否站得住脚。不是真的反对，而是为了把问题想全面。
example: Let me play devil's advocate here — what happens if we invest all this money and the user adoption isn't there?
exampleTranslation: 我唱个反调想想——如果我们投入了这么多钱，但用户不接受怎么办？

expression: What if we look at it this way?
literalTranslation: 如果我们从这个角度看呢？
naturalMeaning: 温和地提出另一个视角，比直接说'you're wrong'有效得多。说服中的经典句式。
example: I hear what you're saying about cost. But what if we look at it this way — this is an investment, not an expense.
exampleTranslation: 我理解你说的成本问题，但如果我们换一个角度呢——这是投资，不是开支。

expression: I'm not asking you to decide right now
literalTranslation: 我不是要你现在就做决定
naturalMeaning: 降低对方的压力，让对方先消化信息再做决定。非常有效的说服技巧。
example: Just take the proposal home and think about it — I'm not asking you to decide right now.
exampleTranslation: 把方案拿回去考虑一下就行，我不是要你现在就做决定。

expression: Walk me through your thinking
naturalMeaning: 请对方解释为什么会有某个看法或决定。'walk me through'非常自然，比'explain your reasoning'更口语化。
example: I'm curious about why you chose that vendor — walk me through your thinking.
exampleTranslation: 我很好奇你为什么选了那个供应商，给我讲讲你的思路。

expression: I'd like to get your take on something
literalTranslation: 我想听听你对某件事的看法
naturalMeaning: 在征求意见时的自然表达。'take'做名词表示看法/立场，是美国口语的典型用法。
example: I'd like to get your take on something — do you think we should pivot or double down on the current strategy?
exampleTranslation: 我想听听你的看法：你觉得我们应该转型还是继续坚持当前的策略？

expression: I'm trying to get ahead of this
literalTranslation: 我想在事情发生之前就提前行动
naturalMeaning: 不等问题发生再处理，而是提前预防。'get ahead of'表示提前应对，是极地道的美式表达。
example: I'm trying to get ahead of this before the client escalates — let's proactively address the delay.
exampleTranslation: 我想在客户升级投诉之前就提前行动，我们主动去沟通延期的事。

expression: This is a win-win
naturalMeaning: 说明某个方案对双方都有利。在谈判和说服中高频使用。
example: If we combine our budgets on this initiative, it's a win-win — we both get the tool we need at half the cost.
exampleTranslation: 如果我们把预算合在一起做这个项目，那是双赢——各花一半的钱得到各自需要的工具。

expression: Let me plant a seed
naturalMeaning: 先提出一个想法让对方慢慢消化，不要求立即同意。是一种长期影响的策略。
example: I'm not going to push this now, but let me plant a seed — what if we open source this component?
exampleTranslation: 我现在不强推，但我先给你种下一颗种子：如果把这个组件开源呢？

expression: I'll circle back with you on that
literalTranslation: 我之后会再回来跟你说这个
naturalMeaning: 表示暂时无法回答或决定，需要后续跟进。'circle back'是美国职场中使用频率极高的短语。
example: I don't have the numbers off the top of my head — let me look it up and circle back with you on that.
exampleTranslation: 我一时想不起具体数字，我查一下再回来跟你说。

expression: Let's take this offline
literalTranslation: 我们把这个讨论带到线下
naturalMeaning: 表示当前的会议不适合讨论这个具体问题，建议会后再单独聊。全美通用的会议用语。
example: This is getting into the weeds — let's take this offline and I'll follow up with an email.
exampleTranslation: 这个问题越来越细了，我们线下再聊，我后续发邮件跟进。
